speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: The gold I gave to Dromio is laid up

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Safe at the Centaur, and the heedful slave

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Is wandered forth in care to seek me out.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: By computation and mine host’s report,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I could not speak with Dromio since at first

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I sent him from the mart. See, here he comes.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: How now, sir? Is your merry humor altered?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: As you love strokes, so jest with me again.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: You know no Centaur? You received no gold?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Your mistress sent to have me home to dinner?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: My house was at the Phoenix? Wast thou mad,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: That thus so madly thou didst answer me?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What answer, sir? When spake I such a word?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Even now, even here, not half an hour since.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I did not see you since you sent me hence,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Home to the Centaur with the gold you gave me.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Villain, thou didst deny the gold’s receipt

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And told’st me of a mistress and a dinner,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: For which I hope thou felt’st I was displeased.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I am glad to see you in this merry vein.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What means this jest, I pray you, master, tell me?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Yea, dost thou jeer and flout me in the teeth?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Think’st thou I jest? Hold, take thou that and that.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Hold, sir, for God’s sake! Now your jest is earnest.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Upon what bargain do you give it me?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Because that I familiarly sometimes

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Do use you for my fool and chat with you,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Your sauciness will jest upon my love

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And make a common of my serious hours.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: When the sun shines, let foolish gnats make sport,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: But creep in crannies when he hides his beams.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: If you will jest with me, know my aspect,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And fashion your demeanor to my looks,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Or I will beat this method in your sconce.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: “Sconce” call you it? So you

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: would leave battering, I had rather have it a

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: “head.” An you use these blows long, I must get a

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: sconce for my head and ensconce it too, or else I

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: shall seek my wit in my shoulders. But I pray, sir,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: why am I beaten?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Dost thou not know?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Nothing, sir, but that I am

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: beaten.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Shall I tell you why?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Ay, sir, and wherefore, for they

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: say every why hath a wherefore.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: “Why” first: for flouting

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: me; and then “wherefore”: for urging it the second

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: time to me.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Was there ever any man thus beaten out of season,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: When in the “why” and the “wherefore” is neither rhyme nor reason?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Well, sir, I thank you.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Thank me, sir, for what?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Marry, sir, for this something

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: that you gave me for nothing.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I’ll make you amends next,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: to give you nothing for something. But say, sir, is it

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: dinnertime?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: No, sir, I think the meat wants

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: that I have.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: In good time, sir, what’s

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: that?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Basting.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Well, sir, then ’twill be dry.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: If it be, sir, I pray you eat none of

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: it.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Your reason?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Lest it make you choleric and

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: purchase me another dry basting.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Well, sir, learn to jest in

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: good time. There’s a time for all things.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I durst have denied that before

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: you were so choleric.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: By what rule, sir?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Marry, sir, by a rule as plain as

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: the plain bald pate of Father Time himself.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Let’s hear it.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: There’s no time for a man to

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: recover his hair that grows bald by nature.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: May he not do it by fine and

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: recovery?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Yes, to pay a fine for a periwig,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: and recover the lost hair of another man.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Why is Time such a niggard

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: of hair, being, as it is, so plentiful an excrement?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Because it is a blessing that he

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: bestows on beasts, and what he hath scanted men

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: in hair, he hath given them in wit.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Why, but there’s many a

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: man hath more hair than wit.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Not a man of those but he hath

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: the wit to lose his hair.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Why, thou didst conclude

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: hairy men plain dealers without wit.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: The plainer dealer, the sooner

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: lost. Yet he loseth it in a kind of jollity.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: For what reason?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: For two, and sound ones too.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Nay, not sound, I pray you.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Sure ones, then.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Nay, not sure, in a thing

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: falsing.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Certain ones, then.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Name them.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: The one, to save the money that

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: he spends in tiring; the other, that at dinner they

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: should not drop in his porridge.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: You would all this time

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: have proved there is no time for all things.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Marry, and did, sir: namely, e’en

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: no time to recover hair lost by nature.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: But your reason was not

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: substantial why there is no time to recover.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Thus I mend it: Time himself is

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: bald and therefore, to the world’s end, will have

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: bald followers.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I knew ’twould be a bald

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: conclusion. But soft, who wafts us yonder?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Ay, ay, Antipholus, look strange and frown.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Some other mistress hath thy sweet aspects.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I am not Adriana, nor thy wife.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: The time was once when thou unurged wouldst vow

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That never words were music to thine ear,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That never object pleasing in thine eye,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That never touch well welcome to thy hand,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That never meat sweet-savored in thy taste,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Unless I spake, or looked, or touched, or carved to thee.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: How comes it now, my husband, O, how comes it

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That thou art then estrangèd from thyself?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: “Thyself” I call it, being strange to me,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That, undividable, incorporate,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Am better than thy dear self’s better part.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Ah, do not tear away thyself from me!

speaker: ADRIANA
text: For know, my love, as easy mayst thou fall

speaker: ADRIANA
text: A drop of water in the breaking gulf,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And take unmingled thence that drop again

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Without addition or diminishing,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: As take from me thyself and not me too.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: How dearly would it touch thee to the quick,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Shouldst thou but hear I were licentious

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And that this body, consecrate to thee,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: By ruffian lust should be contaminate!

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Wouldst thou not spit at me, and spurn at me,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And hurl the name of husband in my face,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And tear the stained skin off my harlot brow,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And from my false hand cut the wedding ring,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And break it with a deep-divorcing vow?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I know thou canst, and therefore see thou do it.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I am possessed with an adulterate blot;

speaker: ADRIANA
text: My blood is mingled with the crime of lust;

speaker: ADRIANA
text: For if we two be one, and thou play false,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I do digest the poison of thy flesh,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Being strumpeted by thy contagion.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Keep then fair league and truce with thy true bed,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I live distained, thou undishonorèd.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Plead you to me, fair dame? I know you not.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: In Ephesus I am but two hours old,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: As strange unto your town as to your talk,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Who, every word by all my wit being scanned,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Wants wit in all one word to understand.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Fie, brother, how the world is changed with you!

speaker: LUCIANA
text: When were you wont to use my sister thus?

speaker: LUCIANA
text: She sent for you by Dromio home to dinner.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: By Dromio?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: By me?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: By thee; and this thou didst return from him:

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That he did buffet thee and, in his blows,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Denied my house for his, me for his wife.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Did you converse, sir, with this gentlewoman?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What is the course and drift of your compact?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I, sir? I never saw her till this time.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Villain, thou liest, for even her very words

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Didst thou deliver to me on the mart.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I never spake with her in all my life.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: How can she thus then call us by our names—

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Unless it be by inspiration?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: How ill agrees it with your gravity

speaker: ADRIANA
text: To counterfeit thus grossly with your slave,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Abetting him to thwart me in my mood.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Be it my wrong you are from me exempt,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: But wrong not that wrong with a more contempt.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Come, I will fasten on this sleeve of thine.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Thou art an elm, my husband, I a vine,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Whose weakness, married to thy stronger state,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Makes me with thy strength to communicate.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: If aught possess thee from me, it is dross,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Usurping ivy, brier, or idle moss,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Who, all for want of pruning, with intrusion

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Infect thy sap and live on thy confusion.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: To me she speaks; she moves me for her theme.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What, was I married to her in my dream?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Or sleep I now and think I hear all this?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What error drives our eyes and ears amiss?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Until I know this sure uncertainty

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I’ll entertain the offered fallacy.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Dromio, go bid the servants spread for dinner.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: O, for my beads! I cross me for a sinner.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: This is the fairy land. O spite of spites!

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: We talk with goblins, owls, and sprites.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: If we obey them not, this will ensue:

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: They’ll suck our breath, or pinch us black and blue.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Why prat’st thou to thyself and answer’st not?

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Dromio—thou, Dromio—thou snail, thou slug, thou sot.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I am transformèd, master, am I not?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I think thou art in mind, and so am I.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Nay, master, both in mind and in my shape.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Thou hast thine own form.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: No, I am an ape.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: If thou art changed to aught, ’tis to an ass.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: ’Tis true. She rides me, and I long for grass.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: ’Tis so. I am an ass; else it could never be

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: But I should know her as well as she knows me.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Come, come, no longer will I be a fool,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: To put the finger in the eye and weep

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Whilst man and master laughs my woes to scorn.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Come, sir, to dinner.—Dromio, keep the gate.—

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Husband, I’ll dine above with you today,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And shrive you of a thousand idle pranks.

speaker: ADRIANA
text:
To
                 
                Dromio
                .
                 
              Sirrah, if any ask you for your master,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Say he dines forth, and let no creature enter.—

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Come, sister.—Dromio, play the porter well.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Am I in Earth, in heaven, or in hell?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Sleeping or waking, mad or well-advised?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Known unto these, and to myself disguised!

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I’ll say as they say, and persever so,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And in this mist at all adventures go.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Master, shall I be porter at the gate?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Ay, and let none enter, lest I break your pate.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Come, come, Antipholus, we dine too late.
